title: Generative AI: A Creative New World
executive_summary: Sequoia's foundational 2022 piece on generative AI argued that we had crossed a threshold from analytical AI to creative AI — machines were beginning to generate sensical and beautiful output, not just classify or predict
The essay mapped the capability progression: text was the most mature layer, followed by code, images, speech, video, and 3D — each unlocking a new wave of applications and business models

The central business thesis: generative AI has the potential to generate trillions of dollars of economic value, and the firms that build durable competitive advantage will do so through a flywheel of user engagement, data, and model improvement within a specific domain
GitHub Copilot already generated nearly 40% of code in projects where it was installed at the time of writing — signalling that AI-augmented knowledge work was not hypothetical but underway

For wealth management, the implication Sequoia identified in 2022 has since arrived: generative AI applied to high-value knowledge work — advice, planning, research — is a multitrillion-dollar opportunity that rewards those who integrate at the data layer, not those who add AI on top
the_one_insight: Written in 2022, this essay identified the framework that explains 2026: generative AI creates its biggest advantages in high-value knowledge work, and the winners build flywheels around proprietary data — not just better models.

key_quotes.text: Generative AI has the potential to generate trillions of dollars of economic value.
key_quotes.context: On the market scale of the generative AI opportunity, written before the current wave

key_quotes.text: GitHub Copilot is now generating nearly 40% of code in the projects where it is installed.
key_quotes.context: Early evidence, cited in 2022, that AI-augmented knowledge work was already materially changing professional productivity
